results.description: Every crate directory must contain a Cargo.toml manifest at its root.
A docs/ directory must exist at the workspace root to house project-level.
The project root must not contain both a build.* script and a bootstrap.*.
Every file and directory at the project root must appear in the configured.
Exactly one architecture.toml may exist — either at the project root or.
Library crates must have a doc/ or docs/ directory for design and API.
Library crates should have an examples/ directory demonstrating canonical.
A bootstrap.sh (or build.sh) must exist at the workspace root to provide.
A bootstrap.ps1 (or build.ps1) must exist at the workspace root for.
Every crate must declare `package.name` in Cargo.toml.
Every crate must declare `package.version` using semantic versioning.
Every crate must declare `package.edition` to pin which Rust language dialect it compiles under.
Every crate must declare a one-line `package.description` stating what it does.
Every crate should declare `package.repository` pointing to its source repository URL.
Every crate should declare `package.authors` listing the people or team responsible for it.
Every crate should declare `package.rust-version` (MSRV) — the minimum Rust toolchain that can compile it.
Library crates should declare up to 5 `package.keywords` to aid discovery on crates.io.
Library crates should declare `package.categories` using valid crates.io category slugs.
A `README.md` must exist at the workspace or project root.
A `CHANGELOG.md` must exist at the workspace root to record notable changes per release.
`.gitignore` at the workspace root must contain a `target/` entry to exclude build artifacts.
The [package].name in Cargo.toml must be all-lowercase kebab-case.
Every .rs source file must use snake_case: all-lowercase, words separated by underscores.
Source file names under main/src/ must contain no uppercase letters.
The configured source directory (default: main/src/) must exist in every.
A src/ directory must not exist inside main/src/ — the path.
A library crate (application_type = 'lib') must not contain a gateway/.
Path construction must use `Path::join` (for host I/O) or a forward-slash-only abstraction (for emitted artifacts) — never `MAIN_SEPARATOR`, literal `\\`, or string-replace hacks.
Every crate must have a tests/ directory at the same level as main/src/ to house its integration and external tests.
api/ types must not expose internal core/ implementation types in their public signatures — parameters, return types, and associated types must all use api/-owned abstractions.
saf/mod.rs and saf/<theme>/mod.rs must not contain `pub use crate::api::` — all api/ re-exports must live in a dedicated _svc.rs file.
mod.rs files must contain only mod declarations and pub use re-exports —.
A SEA layer's mod.rs must not consist solely of wildcard re-exports.
`[dependencies]` must not use `path = '...'` pointing outside the workspace; use git or registry sources.
Every `[[bench]]` target must set `harness = false` when using criterion or a custom main function.
The [lib] path declared in Cargo.toml must point to a file that actually.
Every [[bin]] target declared in Cargo.toml must point to a source file.
Every .rs file inside benches/ must have a corresponding [[bench]] target.
If an examples/ directory exists it must have at least one [[example]].
Every .rs file under tests/ must have a matching [[test]] entry in Cargo.toml so it participates in `cargo test`.
Every .rs file in tests/ must be registered in Cargo.toml; orphan test files that Cargo never compiles must be declared or deleted.
Every [[test]] path declared in Cargo.toml must point to a file that exists on disk.
Cargo.toml must not contain explicit [[test]] entries — Cargo auto-discovers every .rs file directly under tests/ as a test target.
Every non-workspace crate must declare its functional role via `service_type` in `[package.metadata.arch]` so the engine can verify the matching trait and contract are present.
Config struct methods must be pub(crate), not pub — Config is internal wiring resolved at the composition root, not public API.
A crate's saf/ or api/ must not expose another crate's error type in a Result return position — wrap it in a locally owned error type.
Error types returned from api/ trait methods must be declared in api/, never in core/.
Structs with 5+ fields or 3+ `Option<T>` fields should have a companion `*Builder` type to avoid telescoping constructors.
A type name must not repeat its enclosing module name as a prefix.
Package names must not end with a SEA layer suffix (-api, -core, -saf, -spi, -gateway).
Every `mod foo;` declaration must have a corresponding `foo.rs` or `foo/mod.rs` on disk.
Directory names under main/src/ must be snake_case — all lowercase, underscores only.
[[bin]] target names in Cargo.toml must be lowercase with only hyphens or underscores.
Trait names must be singular nouns, not plurals.
Each `.toml` config file must have a corresponding `<Name>ConfigBuilder` struct.
Struct and enum names must not end with `Default` — use the `Default*` prefix instead.
A file or directory name must not begin and end with the same snake_case token (stutter).

File stems and type names must not start with a token from [config.naming] banned_prefix_tokens, or end with one from banned_suffix_tokens (both default to: trait, interface, type, api, core, spi, dto, vo, struct, impl; banned_suffix_tokens also includes ext).
Every workspace member package name must share the common workspace prefix.
Domain (business-logic) crates must not list adapter (infrastructure/IO) crates as Cargo dependencies.
Adapter crates must not define public traits — all port contracts must be owned by the domain crate.
Every adapter crate must implement at least one pub trait declared in a domain crate.
Crate names must not end with catch-all suffixes: -core, -utils, -api, -common, -shared, -helpers.
Every crate must declare `package.license` (SPDX expression) or `package.license-file` (path to a LICENSE file).
Every non-workspace crate must have main/src/lib.rs (library) or.
If Cargo.toml declares [[bench]] targets, a benches/ directory must exist.
No directory path under main/src/ may contain the same segment twice.
Source file names must be nouns — not verb phrases or boolean assertions.
lib.rs must contain only module declarations (mod, pub mod) and.
Every file in a SEA layer directory must contain substantive Rust code —.
lib.rs must declare its submodules explicitly — it must not consist only.
Each SEA layer directory must meet a minimum lines-of-code threshold:.
The [lib] path and all [[bin]] paths in Cargo.toml must point to files.
A non-standard [lib] path and a main/src/ directory must not coexist —.
Every entry in `[dependencies]` must be imported somewhere in `main/src/`; test-only deps belong in `[dev-dependencies]`.
Every test file in tests/ must end in a recognised category suffix so the test kind is unambiguous from the filename.
Every test file's name prefix must resolve to a real source module in main/src/, optionally followed by a public function name from that module.
A test file with a layer prefix (e.g. core_scanner_int_test.rs) must have a corresponding source file at main/src/<layer>/scanner.rs (or scanner/mod.rs).
Any test file that uses the _happy scenario suffix must also contain at least one _error or _edge test — happy-path-only coverage with scenario labels is incomplete.
A #[test] function body must not consist solely of panic!, todo!(), unimplemented!(), or be empty — such tests cannot fail and prove nothing.
An assertion must not compare a value to itself (assert_eq!(x.is_ok(), x.is_ok())), because such comparisons are always true and catch no bug.
A test that only checks is_ok() or is_some() — with no payload assertion and no negative counterpart — is too weak to catch wrong-value bugs.
Every #[test] function must contain at least one assertion macro (assert!, assert_eq!, assert_ne!, assert!(matches!(...))) or be annotated #[should_panic].
The pipeline measures line coverage with cargo llvm-cov; the package must reach the threshold in config.dynamic_checks.coverage_min_line_pct.

result.reason: dynamic check — enforced by the pipeline, not static audit

results.description: cargo-mutants must report no more than config.dynamic_checks.mutation_max_missed surviving mutants; each surviving mutant is a line that tests never falsify.

result.reason: dynamic check — enforced by the pipeline, not static audit

results.description: Test functions in tests/<file>.rs must follow <feature>_<type_kind>_<fn>_<suffix>; functions in tests/<feature>/<file>.rs drop the feature prefix.
Every #[test] function name must either start with test_ or end in a recognised category suffix (_test, _int_test, _e2e_test, _perf_test, etc.).
Files in api/, saf/, and gateway/ must not have inline #[cfg(test)] blocks that test public functions — those tests must live in external tests/ files.
Any #[test] function in main/src/ that is not inside a #[cfg(test)] block is test code leaking into the production build.
Every pub fn in main/src/ must have at least one test function whose name contains the function's name.
Every #[test] function must have a `/// @covers: fn_name` doc comment linking it to the production function it exercises.
Files named *_int_test.rs must not import mock libraries (mockall, mockito, etc.) or use generated Mock* types — integration tests must exercise real implementations.
Every external crate listed in [dependencies] that is used in main/src/ must have at least one *_int_test.rs or *_e2e_test.rs file that imports and exercises it.
A test file's suffix must match its actual dependency scope: _test.rs for same-module imports only, _e2e_test.rs for intra-crate imports, _int_test.rs for external crate imports.
Library code must not call `println!` or `eprintln!`; use `tracing::info!` / `warn!` / `error!` instead.
Source files must not use `#[allow(unused_imports)]` to silence the lint; the import must be removed instead.
`include_str!` embedding a config or policy file must be paired with a runtime `std::fs::read_to_string` path; the embed is a fallback, not the sole source.
`.unwrap()` calls in production source are forbidden; use `?`, `match`, or `unwrap_or_else` instead.
`.expect('')` or `.expect('TODO')` (empty or placeholder messages) are forbidden; provide a meaningful invariant description or use `?`.
`panic!()` calls are forbidden in library code; return `Result<_, E>` and propagate errors instead.
`Cargo.toml` must configure `[lints.clippy]` with `unwrap_used` and `expect_used` set to `'deny'` or `'warn'`.
`#[allow(dead_code)]` and `#[expect(dead_code)]` are forbidden in production source; dead items must be deleted or wired in.
CLI binaries must resolve user-specific directories (config, data, cache) through the `dirs` crate rather than constructing paths manually.
Source code must not contain literal home-directory path segments (`/home/`, `~/`, `/Users/`, `C:\Users\`).
Production functions in main/src/ must not have stub bodies consisting of todo!(), unimplemented!(), or an empty body — these panic at runtime and are never safe to ship.
Source files must not contain literal credential values — API keys, passwords, tokens, private keys, or connection strings with embedded credentials.
Every project must have a `SECURITY.md` (or `.github/SECURITY.md`) documenting how to report vulnerabilities and which versions are supported.
Every `unsafe { ... }` block must be immediately preceded by a `// SAFETY:` comment that explains why the operation is sound.
The project must have `cargo-deny` (via `deny.toml`) or `cargo audit` wired into CI to scan dependencies against the RustSec advisory database.
Every crate must declare its architectural role via `application_type` in `[package.metadata.arch]` so the engine knows which SEA layer rules apply.
lib.rs must not declare api as a public module — all layer modules must be private; consumers access types only via pub use saf::*.
api/ files must not import from core/ (use crate::core::) — L2 depends only on L1 abstractions, never on L3 implementations.
L2 domain primitive crates must not have Cargo.toml dependencies on L3+ assembler-layer crates.
api/ must not import from core/ — api/ defines abstractions only and must be self-contained (mirrors api_no_core_import but with test-code exclusions).
api/ must not import from spi/ — api/ is the innermost layer and must be independent of all implementation layers, including extension points.
api/ must not import from gateway/ — api/ is the innermost layer, gateway/ is the outermost; depending on it completely inverts the layer stack.
Types in api/<theme_a>/types/ must not be referenced in trait files from a different theme api/<theme_b>/traits/ — cross-theme type coupling violates domain isolation.

Every type and error declared in api/ must appear in at least one native trait method signature — orphan types with no trait usage are dead weight. Scans every api/ declaration, not just types/vo/errors/ subdirectories, and exempts concrete trait implementors (impl Trait for Self in core/spi) regardless of where they're declared.

violations.message: 'BackoffSchedule' is declared in api/ but not referenced by any trait file — remove it or add a trait that uses it
violations.expected: referenced in at least one api/traits/ file
violations.fix_hint: Add the type as a method parameter or return type in a pub trait in api/traits/ (or api/<theme>/traits/), remove the unused type declaration, or confirm it's a genuine trait implementor (impl Trait for Self in core/spi) — implementors are exempt. A bare 'pub use' re-export in traits/mod.rs is not sufficient.
violations.llm_prompt:
WHAT: Every type and error declared in api/ must appear in at least one native trait method signature — orphan types with no trait usage are dead weight.
WHY: An orphaned type has no role in the contract; it confuses readers who wonder which trait uses it, and is likely left over from a refactoring that removed the only trait reference.
BEFORE (violation):
  // api/types/analysis_result.rs
  pub struct AnalysisResult { ... } // declared but never appears in any api/traits/ method signature
AFTER (compliant):
  // api/traits/analyzer.rs
  pub trait Analyzer { fn analyze(&self, input: &str) -> AnalysisResult; } // type now has a home
DECISION:
  - Search api/traits/**/*.rs (not mod.rs) for the type name in method signatures, return types, or associated type bounds
  - pub use re-exports in traits/mod.rs do NOT satisfy this rule; only actual method signatures count
  - If no trait needs it: delete the file and remove from mod.rs; run cargo check
EDGE CASES:
  - Error types in Result<T, MyError> return positions: the method return type counts — MyError is not orphaned
  - Types used only in core/ or saf/: they still need a trait signature in api/ to satisfy this rule
  - A pub struct/enum that never appears in a trait signature but has `impl SomeOwnTrait for Self` in core/ or spi/ is a concrete trait implementor, not an orphan — exempt regardless of directory

results.description: A pub trait declared in api/ must live under a traits/ (or <theme>/traits/) subdirectory — traits declared elsewhere cause no_orphan_types to misreport the types they reference as unused.
A type declared under api/<theme>/vo/ (or api/vo/) must not implement a trait in core/ or spi/ — vo/ holds plain-data value objects; concrete trait implementors belong elsewhere in api/.
A core/ impl block must not implement a trait from api/<theme_a>/ for a type from api/<theme_b>/ — the impl must live within the type's own theme.
api/ struct fields must be trait objects (Arc<dyn Trait> or Box<dyn Trait>) or value types — not concrete domain structs from other layers.
api/types/ and api/errors/ files must not import from api/traits/ — VOs and DTOs are pure data; importing a domain trait signals the type carries behavior and belongs in core/.
Every api/<theme>/ directory that has a types/ subdirectory must also have a traits/ subdirectory with at least one natively-declared pub trait (not a re-export).
The api/ layer root must contain only mod.rs — no loose .rs files and no.
The terminal leaf directories of the api/ layer (types/, traits/, errors/).
Every pub trait declared in api/ must have at least one `impl Trait for Struct` block in core/.
Every core/ struct that exposes pub or pub(crate) methods must implement at least one trait declared in api/.
For every subdirectory under core/, there must be a corresponding module in api/ containing real trait or type declarations (not just re-exports).

violations.message: main\src\core\backoff_scheduler.rs has no corresponding api interface counterpart
violations.fix_hint: Create 'main\src\api\backoff_scheduler.rs' with type/trait definitions that main\src\core\backoff_scheduler.rs implements against
violations.llm_prompt:
WHAT: For every subdirectory under core/, there must be a corresponding module in api/ containing real trait or type declarations (not just re-exports).

WHY: Without an api/ counterpart, the core/ module has no declared contract to program against. Implementation precedes abstraction, inverting the SEA dependency direction.

BEFORE (violating):
  core/parser/   ← exists
  api/           ← no parser/ module  ❌

AFTER (compliant):
  api/parser/
    mod.rs       ← declares pub trait Parser, pub struct ParseOptions  ✅
  core/parser/
    mod.rs       ← impl Parser for DefaultParser

EDGE CASES:
- A stub api/ module that only does `pub use other_module::Foo` does NOT satisfy this rule; the api/ module must own at least one real declaration.
- Utility/helper submodules in core/ that have no public-facing types may be exempt — check if the violations reference a genuine domain module.

results.description: Every pub trait, every method in a pub trait, and every pub struct/enum in api/types/ or api/errors/ must have a `///` doc comment.
api/ must not reference external crate types in type positions (struct fields, fn parameters, return types) — wrap them in a local newtype declared in api/types/.
Every pub trait in api/ must have at least one `&self` or `&mut self` method to be usable as `dyn Trait` at runtime.
Traits named *Factory must not be declared as pub in api/ because they are static constructor namespaces, not injectable port contracts.
The E in `Result<T, E>` for pub trait methods in api/ must be a named local error enum — String, &str, Box<dyn Error>, anyhow::Error, and eyre::Report are all banned.
The T in `Result<T, E>` for pub trait methods in api/ should be a named struct from api/types/, not a primitive (String, u64, bool, etc.).
In api/ trait methods returning `Result<T, E>`, BOTH T and E must be named local types — a mixed shape (named T, opaque E, or vice versa) is banned.
Every `pub fn` with a `self` receiver in an api/ pub trait must return `Result<T, E>` — infallible signatures are banned because they hide the failure contract.
Every `pub fn` with a `self` receiver in an api/ pub trait must accept exactly one parameter whose type ends with `Request`, declared in api/types/.
The T in `Result<T, E>` for pub trait methods in api/ must end with `Response` or be `()` for side-effect-only operations.
The E in `Result<T, E>` for pub trait methods in api/ must end with `Error` — non-`*Error` names obscure the failure contract.
api/ files must not contain impl blocks — api/ is a pure declaration layer (traits, types, error structs; no logic).
Only pub trait contracts from api/ may cross the crate boundary — lib.rs must delegate via saf/, saf/ must not re-export concrete types, and all layer mod paths must be private.
Enums in api/ must be in api/types/ or be pub(crate) — a bare pub enum in the wrong subdirectory exposes an unsealed variant set to all downstream pattern matchers.
Type aliases in api/ must not expose internal core/ types — use a newtype struct or opaque impl Trait instead of a transparent alias.
For every pub trait declared in api/, there must be a corresponding <trait_snake>_svc_factory.rs file in saf/.
Every method on every pub trait in api/ must have three test functions in tests/: one _happy, one _error, and one _edge — exercised through a test-double implementation of the trait.
Every submodule declared in api/mod.rs must have a matching submodule declaration in core/mod.rs.
No .rs files (other than lib.rs/main.rs) may sit directly at the.
SEA layer files must not contain free-standing fn — all functions must be methods on a type declared in api/ or the same layer.
Directory names inside SEA layers (api/, core/, saf/, spi/, gateway/) must not contain underscores.
api/ must not contain bare pub struct outside designated homes — api/error/ or api/<domain>/errors/ for error types; api/types/ or api/<domain>/types/ for value objects.
The api/ layer must contain at least one traits/ subdirectory holding real pub trait declarations.
The api/ layer must contain at least one error/ subdirectory holding the crate's public error types.
`api/traits.rs` must contain a `pub trait <PrimaryTrait>` whose name matches the declared `service_type` (e.g. `service_type = 'validator'` → `pub trait Validator`).
At least one struct in `core/` must implement the primary api trait declared for this crate's `service_type`.
Every non-orchestrator crate must declare a `pub trait Validator` in `api/traits.rs` that checks whether its input is structurally valid before the primary operation runs.
Deprecated traits in `api/traits.rs` are surfaced so they can be removed after consumers migrate to the replacement.
All public types (struct, enum, trait, type alias) must be declared in api/ — no other layer is a legal declaration site.
SEA layer directories (api/, core/, saf/, spi/) must not contain a.
Every public type, trait, or enum in api/ must live in its own file whose stem matches the type's snake_case name.
Files and types in api/ must not use the Default* prefix — that prefix is reserved for core/ implementation variants.
Each public struct, trait, or enum must live in its own .rs file named.
Sibling .rs files sharing a common prefix must be grouped into a.

violations.message: 2 files in main/src/core/ share prefix 'backoff': backoff_schedule, backoff_scheduler — group into main/src/core/backoff/
violations.fix_hint: Create a subdirectory named after the shared prefix and move files into it (e.g., `check_runner.rs` → `check/runner.rs`)
violations.llm_prompt:
WHAT: Sibling .rs files sharing a common prefix must be grouped into a
subdirectory named after that prefix.

WHY: A shared prefix is a signal that the files form a cohesive concern.
Leaving them as siblings obscures the grouping — the directory does not
communicate what belongs together. Promoting them to a subdirectory makes
the module boundary explicit and discoverable.

BEFORE (violating):
  core/
    check_runner.rs   // ❌ — shared prefix
    check_result.rs   // ❌
    check_config.rs   // ❌

AFTER (compliant):
  core/
    check/
      mod.rs          // pub(crate) mod runner; mod result; mod config;  ✅
      runner.rs
      result.rs
      config.rs

EDGE CASES:
- Strip the prefix from the filename when moving: check_runner.rs → check/runner.rs.
- If only two files share a prefix and one is a mod.rs, the grouping is already
  correct; do not over-nest.
- Update all `use crate::core::check_runner::` paths to `use crate::core::check::runner::`.

results.description: Every public function in api/ must be verified by an external test file in tests/; inline #[cfg(test)] blocks in api/ source files are not sufficient for public functions.
Every pub trait declared in api/traits.rs must have a corresponding test file in tests/ (e.g. tests/foo_int_test.rs for trait Foo).
Every source file in api/, core/, saf/, and spi/ must be covered by at least one of: inline #[cfg(test)] block, a matching test file, or a layer-level test file.
Files in saf/ and api/ must not contain any #[cfg(test)] block — all tests for these layers must live in standalone tests/ files.
Test files for api/ and saf/ layers cannot `use crate::core::` or `use crate::spi::` directly — they must test through the public api:: interface.
For each api/ domain, tests must exist in exactly one location: either api/<domain>/tests/ (colocated) or tests/<domain>_* (external) — never both.

Every .rs file under api/<domain>/traits/ (except mod.rs) must have a corresponding <trait>_e2e_test.rs in the configured test location. Stand-alone structural dirs (api/types/, api/errors/) are exempt.
Colocated test files must live at api/<domain>/tests/<trait>_e2e_test.rs — never inside structural leaf directories (traits/tests/, types/tests/, errors/tests/).
The on-disk directory structure inside `main/src/` must match what the declared `application_type` requires.
